# Vendor Context

## Objective
Build reliable subcontractor fulfillment around standardized scopes, preset package buy rates, response SLAs, photo proof, callback tracking, and redundancy.

## Vendor Model
Ready White does not operate around flat hourly labor. Vendor fulfillment should use:
- Preset package buy rates.
- Standard size bands.
- Standard add-ons.
- Exception escalation workflows.

Hourly or change-order rates are only for approved exceptions such as severe prep, water damage, smoke or stain blocking, custom repairs, and other documented scope exceptions.

## Dispatch Requirements
Every dispatch should include:
- Customer and property details.
- Unit access instructions.
- Room list and package scope.
- Included surfaces.
- Excluded surfaces.
- Approved add-ons.
- Required completion window.
- Required before, in-progress, and after photos.
- Callback and quality expectations.

## Vendor Scorecards
Track:
- Response time.
- Acceptance rate.
- On-time arrival.
- On-time completion.
- Photo proof compliance.
- Scope accuracy.
- Callback rate.
- Customer satisfaction.
- Property-manager satisfaction.

## Operational Redundancy
Maintain backup vendors by service area and package type. Do not allow one vendor dependency to jeopardize vacancy turnover time or recurring property-management relationships.
